content.Text:
[Mon 2026-04-20 07:37 PDT] Create a new interactive HTML tool at docs/time-machine.html for the GraphVisual project. This is a "Graph Time Machine" - a version control system for graphs with visual diffing and proactive structural regression detection.

## What to Build

A single self-contained HTML file (no external dependencies) with:

### 1. Graph Editor (left panel)
- Canvas-based graph editor where users can add/remove nodes and edges by clicking
- Drag nodes to reposition
- Click canvas to add node, click two nodes to add edge, right-click to delete
- 8 preset graphs (Petersen, Complete K5, Cycle C8, Star S6, Grid 3x3, Binary Tree, Barbell, Random)

### 2. Snapshot System
- "Take Snapshot" button saves current graph state with auto-generated name (v1, v2, ...)
- Snapshot list panel showing all versions with timestamp
- Click any snapshot to restore it
- Each snapshot stores: nodes (id, x, y, label), edges, and computed metrics

### 3. Visual Diff View (right panel)
- Select two snapshots to compare
- Canvas rendering showing both graphs overlaid:
  - Green = added nodes/edges
  - Red = removed nodes/edges  
  - Gray = unchanged
- Diff summary table: nodes added/removed, edges added/removed, metrics changed

### 4. Structural Metrics Tracked Per Snapshot
- Node count, edge count, density
- Connected components count
- Average degree
- Diameter (BFS-based, -1 if disconnected)
- Clustering coefficient (global)

### 5. Proactive Regression Detection (AGENTIC)
- After each snapshot, auto-compare with previous snapshot
- Detect and alert on:
  - Connectivity loss (components increased)
  - Density drop > 20%
  - Diameter increase (path efficiency degraded)
  - Clustering coefficient drop > 30%
- Show alerts as colored badges in the snapshot list
- "Health Timeline" chart at bottom showing metrics over snapshots as sparklines

### 6. Export
- Export snapshot history as JSON
- Import JSON to restore full history

## Style
- Dark theme matching the project (background: #1a1a2e, accent: #6c63ff, cards: #16213e)
- Clean modern UI with CSS grid layout
- Responsive
- Title: "Graph Time Machine" with clock icon ⏰

## Technical Notes
- All in one HTML file, pure vanilla JS, no frameworks
- Canvas for graph rendering
- Use adjacency list internally
- BFS for diameter, standard formulas for other metrics
- Snapshot stored in JS array (in-memory)

## IMPORTANT
- Do NOT create any other files
- Do NOT modify any existing files
- Only create docs/time-machine.html
- After creating the file, run: python -c "import html.parser; print('HTML syntax OK')" to verify it's valid-ish
